title: Manager-facing team capability platform (product definition)
requirement: Extend the individual companion into a manager-facing platform where a manager can understand, improve, and distribute AI capability across their team, answering six manager questions (team capability level; strong/weak/improving/declining capabilities; who needs coaching; who can mentor; where expertise is concentrated; is spend producing growth).

title: Usage is an input; capability is the outcome
requirement: The product must not reduce team intelligence to activity volume, token consumption, or cost; usage data is an input and capability development is the product outcome.

title: Principle: capability over activity
requirement: High AI usage must not automatically imply high capability; capability is judged on patterns (context quality, iteration, verification, workflow depth, tool selection, reuse, growth), not volume.

title: Principle: growth over ranking, no leaderboards
requirement: Emphasize improvement, readiness, coaching, and strengths rather than employee competition; avoid public leaderboards and simplistic rankings.

title: Principle: explainability over opaque scoring
requirement: Every capability score must expose contributing signals, confidence level, recent changes, supporting evidence, known data limitations, and a recommended next action; scores never shown without confidence + evidence availability.

title: Principle: coaching over surveillance; no prompt content by default
requirement: Manager insights should lead to development actions, not punitive monitoring; sensitive prompt content must not be exposed by default.

title: Principle: universal core + role packs across 10+ job functions
requirement: Revealyst should work across engineering, product, marketing, sales, customer success, operations, finance, HR, design, legal and other knowledge-work teams — a shared universal capability model plus role packs adding domain-specific criteria.

title: Principle: capability and cost remain separate concepts
requirement: Cost is an operational metric, not evidence of capability; low spend is not inherently good and high spend not inherently bad; cost never enters capability scoring.

title: MVP: team/workspace structure + manager permissions
requirement: MVP must-haves include team creation and membership and manager permissions (managers distinct from workspace admins; sub-teams/subgroups within a workspace).

title: MVP: manager-facing per-person surfaces (Capability Matrix, Team Members, individual profile, Coaching Center, Skills & Experts)
requirement: MVP must-haves include a Capability Matrix (members × capabilities), a Team Members roster with per-person capability, a manager-facing individual profile, a Coaching Center where managers assign coaching to individuals, and a Skills & Experts directory naming individuals.

title: MVP: aggregate team surfaces (Team Overview, Growth, insight feed, confidence disclosures)
requirement: MVP must-haves include a Team Overview, a team Growth screen, a manager insight feed, and confidence/data-quality disclosures.

title: MVP: team + individual cost analytics
requirement: MVP must-haves include individual and team cost analytics, with admin-controlled visibility of individual cost to managers.

title: MVP: manager reports (weekly team brief + monthly capability review)
requirement: MVP must-haves include weekly and monthly manager reports summarizing changes, risks, coaching priorities, emerging experts, and data-quality issues.

title: MVP 'Should Have': mentor matching, concentration risk, campaigns, benchmarks, role packs
requirement: Should-haves include mentor matching, knowledge-concentration risk, coaching campaigns, benchmark comparisons, saved views, scheduled reports, and role-specific packs.

title: MVP 'Later' bucket alignment (external benchmarks, forecasting, custom frameworks, business-outcome linkage, org-wide)
requirement: Defer external benchmarks, capability forecasting, custom capability frameworks, advanced coaching-impact analysis, business-outcome linkage, and organization-wide intelligence to a later phase.

title: Initial release starts with the Engineering role pack
requirement: The first implementation should use one universal framework + the Engineering role pack, because existing data sources are strongest for developer workflows.

title: Role-agnostic architecture so future packs slot in without redesign
requirement: The architecture and UI must remain role-agnostic so additional role packs can be added without redesigning the team product.

title: Initial release avoids ROI/productivity claims, rankings, opaque efficiency scores, invalid benchmarks, causal coaching-impact
requirement: The initial release must avoid ROI claims, productivity claims, employee rankings, opaque efficiency scores, external benchmarking without valid comparison data, and automatic coaching-impact causality claims.

title: Reposition as 'AI Capability Intelligence & Growth Platform' with a manager layer
requirement: Position Revealyst as an AI Capability Intelligence and Growth Platform, with Team Intelligence as the manager layer on top of individual growth.

title: Individual growth = foundation; team = manager layer; cost supports but does not replace capability
requirement: Individual growth remains the foundation, Team Intelligence is the manager layer, and cost analytics supports operational understanding without replacing capability development as the central value.

title: Four-role permission model
requirement: Support four distinct roles — Individual Contributor, Team Manager, Workspace Administrator, Executive/Observer — each with its own data-access scope.

title: Manager sees named per-person capability profile by default
requirement: A team manager can, by default, open a named team member's individual capability profile (universal + role-pack breakdown, evidence, trend).

title: Manager sees individual (named) cost analytics
requirement: Managers can see per-person monthly cost, model mix, premium-model share, token volume, and efficiency classification for named members.

title: Manager notes entity
requirement: Managers can write private notes on a team member (author, timestamp, optional follow-up date), manager-private, that never affect calculated scores.

title: Admin toggle: individual cost visible to managers
requirement: Workspace administrators control whether individual cost data is visible to managers.

title: Granular Team Settings visibility toggles
requirement: Configure independently: whether members see team aggregates, whether managers see individual cost, whether prompt-level evidence is available, whether mentor recommendations are visible, whether notes are manager-private.

title: Prompt-level evidence availability toggle
requirement: Configure whether prompt-level evidence is available; raw prompt content hidden by default.

title: Raw prompt content hidden by default
requirement: Managers see behavioral summaries, not raw private conversations, by default.

title: Role-scoped name visibility (managers see names, ICs see pseudonyms)
requirement: Real names are visible to managers while individual contributors continue to see pseudonyms / no named peer scores.

title: Distinguish observed behavior vs score vs inference vs note vs missing data
requirement: The system clearly distinguishes observed behavior, calculated score, inferred capability, manager-provided note, and incomplete/unavailable data.

title: Users understand what data contributes to their profile
requirement: Users can see what data contributes to their capability profile.

title: Data-confidence disclosure wherever telemetry is incomplete
requirement: Data-confidence disclosures are visible wherever incomplete telemetry materially affects interpretation.

title: No implication of performance/productivity/employment quality
requirement: The interface must not imply performance, productivity, or employment quality from AI capability, and must avoid ROI/productivity/ranking claims.

title: Users cannot access unauthorized individual data
requirement: Users cannot access unauthorized individual data; ICs do not see other members' private scores/costs/notes/activity unless permitted; named peer scores hidden by default.

title: Executive/Observer read-only aggregate role
requirement: An optional read-only Executive/Observer role can access aggregate team trends without individual-level evidence or prompt data.

title: Configurable retention & privacy settings
requirement: Workspace administrators can configure retention and privacy settings.

title: Admin configures permissions & assigns managers
requirement: Workspace administrators can assign managers and configure permissions.

title: Team entity inside a workspace
requirement: A named Team that lives inside a workspace and groups its members, so managers can view and act on one team at a time.

title: Manager role and per-team manager assignment
requirement: A 'manager' concept — a person/user assigned to manage one or more teams, with elevated access to that team's data — and an admin workflow to assign managers.

title: Manager-facing per-member capability view
requirement: Managers can open any team member's individual capability profile, scores, evidence, and history.

title: Individual-contributor self-view within a team org
requirement: In a team workspace, each member sees their own capability profile, recommendations, coaching, and permitted team aggregates.

title: Executive / Observer read-only aggregate role
requirement: An optional read-only role that sees aggregate team trends only, with no individual-level evidence or prompt data.

title: Sub-teams / subgroups (nested team hierarchy)
requirement: Teams can contain subgroups (squads/locations), and views can group/filter by subgroup.

title: Workspace-admin team management and permission configuration
requirement: Workspace admins can create/manage teams, assign managers, configure per-area permissions, enable role packs, and set visibility of cost/prompt evidence.

title: Team Intelligence top-level nav area (11 sub-items)
requirement: Add a top-level 'Team Intelligence' area with Overview, Capability Matrix, Team Members, Growth, Coaching, Skills & Experts, Costs & Efficiency, Insights, Benchmarks, Reports, Team Settings; hide manager-only items from non-managers.

title: Manager-only navigation gating
requirement: Users without manager/admin permission must not see manager-only nav items.

title: Team entity data shape (managerIds, memberIds, rolePackIds, settings)
requirement: Team = { id, workspaceId, name, managerIds[], memberIds[], rolePackIds[], settings }.

title: RolePack entity (versioned capability bundles, per-team enablement)
requirement: RolePack = { id, name, capabilityIds[], version, status: draft|active|deprecated }, enabled per team, adding role-specific capabilities on top of the universal model.

title: Per-team Capability Configuration (enable caps, importance, custom labels/dimensions)
requirement: Per team: choose enabled universal capabilities and role packs, set capability importance weights, custom skill labels, and custom capability dimensions.

title: TeamCapabilitySnapshot entity
requirement: A stored per-period team snapshot { teamId, periodStart/End, overallScore, capabilityScores, maturityDistribution, confidence, represented/totalMemberCount }.

title: CapabilityDefinition universal vs role_specific categorization
requirement: CapabilityDefinition carries category 'universal'|'role_specific', scoringVersion, and evidenceTypes.

title: New team-layer entities require frozen-contract ADR + three registrations
requirement: Any new org-scoped table (team_managers, per-team settings, subgroups, snapshots, role-pack mappings) must be added via the frozen-contract process.

title: Per-capability score object (CapabilityScore)
requirement: Every capability score for a person uses one common structure: capabilityId, userId, score, level, trend, change, confidence, confidenceScore, evidenceCount, positiveSignals, limitingSignals, dataLimitations, lastCalculatedAt.

title: Five-level capability ladder (inactive→expert)
requirement: level is one of inactive | beginner | intermediate | advanced | expert.

title: Per-capability trend and change (period-over-period)
requirement: Each capability score carries trend (improving|stable|declining|insufficient_data) and change (numeric delta), and team growth charts show capability over time.

title: Confidence always shown; low/medium/high band
requirement: A capability score is never shown without confidence and evidence availability; confidence renders as low|medium|high (+ insufficient-data).

title: Manager-visible per-person capability score
requirement: CapabilityScore.userId identifies a person and managers can view individual team-member capability profiles (§3.2, §6.4).

title: Team entity (Team)
requirement: Team = { id, workspaceId, name, managerIds[], memberIds[], rolePackIds[], settings }.

title: TeamSettings persistence
requirement: Per-team configuration: enabled universal capabilities, enabled role packs, capability importance, custom skill labels, visibility toggles (member aggregates, individual cost, prompt evidence, mentor recs, note privacy), concentration thresholds, benchmark config.

title: RolePack entity
requirement: RolePack = { id, name, capabilityIds[], version, status(draft|active|deprecated) } grouping role-specific capabilities; packs for Engineering, Product, Marketing, Sales, CS, HR, Finance, Operations.

title: CapabilityDefinition entity
requirement: CapabilityDefinition = { id, name, description, category(universal|role_specific), scoringVersion, evidenceTypes[] }.

title: TeamCapabilitySnapshot entity
requirement: TeamCapabilitySnapshot = { id, teamId, periodStart, periodEnd, overallScore, capabilityScores(Record), maturityDistribution(Record), confidence, representedMemberCount, totalMemberCount }.

title: CostAllocation entity
requirement: CostAllocation = { userId?, teamId, provider, model?, period, amount, currency, confidence, allocationMethod } with allocated-vs-unallocated spend and allocation confidence.

title: Background calculation jobs for team entities
requirement: Background jobs: individual capability recalculation, team aggregation, maturity distribution, growth calculation, cost allocation, insight generation (plus skill/mentor/concentration generation).

title: Auditability record
requirement: Store scoring version, calculation timestamp, evidence categories, confidence inputs, changes between score versions, manager actions, and dismissed recommendations.

title: AI Adoption dimension signals
requirement: Compute an AI Adoption universal dimension from usage frequency, workflow breadth, tool/model exploration, continued use over time, and progression beyond basic chat.

title: AI Communication dimension signals
requirement: Score how effectively a user communicates objectives, context, constraints, output structure, and examples to AI (clarity/completeness/constraint/structure/examples).

title: AI Thinking dimension signals
requirement: Score use of AI for reasoning, research, planning, decomposition, option comparison, and synthesis.

title: AI Iteration dimension signals
requirement: Score refinement through feedback: follow-up depth, correction behaviour, iterative refinement, reuse of outputs, sustained task progression.

title: AI Verification dimension signals
requirement: Score how consistently a user validates AI output: fact-checking, testing, source requests, cross-checking, uncertainty handling, review before use.

title: AI Workflow Integration dimension signals
requirement: Score how deeply AI is embedded in repeatable work: saved workflows, reusable prompts, templates, connected tools, automations, agent/multi-step workflows.

title: AI Learning Velocity dimension signals
requirement: Score the rate of capability improvement: score change over time, recommendation adoption, new-capability acquisition, movement to advanced workflows, weakness reduction.

title: Engineering role-pack capability feasibility
requirement: Provide Engineering-pack capabilities: code generation, debugging, testing, architecture reasoning, code review, context engineering, agentic development, dev-tool integration.

title: Non-engineering role packs (Product/Marketing/Sales/CS/HR/Finance/Operations) telemetry
requirement: Provide role packs and universal-dimension scores for product, marketing, sales, customer success, HR, finance, and operations teams.

title: Team Settings → Data Sources health panel
requirement: Show connected providers, sync status, last sync, and data freshness in team settings.

title: Member matching status + allocation-gap disclosure
requirement: Show member matching status and allocation/coverage gaps for team data sources.

title: Cost is never folded into capability scores
requirement: Capability/maturity scoring must never include spend as an input; cost stays a separate operational metric.

title: No opaque efficiency score; explainable classifications first
requirement: Do not launch a single opaque 'AI Efficiency Score'; use explainable classifications, each of which explains why it was assigned.

title: Team Cost Overview (aggregate spend, breakdowns, allocation confidence)
requirement: Show total spend, period change, spend by provider/tool, spend by model, allocated vs unallocated spend, and cost-data confidence.

title: Spend by model shown as dollars
requirement: Break spend down by model.

title: Per-member / per-role cost table visible to managers
requirement: Give managers a per-member cost table (monthly spend, spend change, premium-model share, active days, efficiency classification, allocation confidence) and spend by role/by team member.

title: Honest cost-insight narratives (growth-vs-spend)
requirement: Produce plain-English cost insights (e.g. 'premium-model usage increased but capability growth stayed stable', 'low spend because adoption is low', 'growth without a spend increase', 'large share of spend unallocated').

title: Cost insights must not recommend cutting access solely for being expensive
requirement: Never frame cost reduction as the default objective; never recommend reducing a user's access just because they are expensive.

title: §15 Activation metrics for the team product
requirement: Measure % of workspaces creating a team, % of managers opening Team Overview, % of teams with sufficient data coverage, % of managers viewing Capability Matrix.

title: §15 Engagement metrics (manager active, insight opens, coaching action, report views, expert searches)
requirement: Measure weekly manager active rate, insight-card open rate, coaching recommendation action rate, report-view rate, and expert-directory searches.

title: §15 Capability-outcome metrics
requirement: Measure improvement in weak team capabilities, increase in advanced-capability coverage, reduction in knowledge concentration, recommendation completion, and sustained capability growth.

title: §15 Trust metrics (confidence coverage, explanation usage, dismissal, disputes, disclosure rate)
requirement: Measure % of scores at medium/high confidence, score-explanation usage, manager dismissal rate for inaccurate insights, user disputes/corrections, and incomplete-data disclosure rate.

title: §15 Cost-outcome metrics
requirement: Measure allocated-spend coverage, reduction in unnecessary premium-model usage, capability growth relative to spend, and number of actionable cost insights.

title: Individual capability recalculation job
requirement: A background job recalculates each person's capability scores on a cadence.

title: Team aggregation + team capability snapshot persistence
requirement: A job aggregates member capability into a team-level view (TeamCapabilitySnapshot: overallScore, per-capability scores, maturity distribution, represented vs total member count) and stores period snapshots.

title: Maturity/capability distribution by member counts
requirement: A job computes member counts by maturity level (inactive/beginner/intermediate/advanced/expert) per capability and for the team overall.

title: Growth / movement calculation
requirement: A job computes capability change over time: velocity, movement up/down a level, plateau, and decline counts.

title: Skill extraction job
requirement: A job detects/extracts discrete AI-related skills per person (prompt design, verification, automation, specific tools, role-specific workflows) with proficiency and evidence recency.

title: Mentor matching job
requirement: A job matches learners to internal mentors per capability with a mentor-confidence score and workload/conflict warnings.

title: Concentration-risk detection (per-capability, configurable thresholds)
requirement: A job flags capabilities where expertise is concentrated in one or two members, with configurable risk thresholds (low/moderate/high/critical).

title: Coaching recommendation generation (manager-facing)
requirement: A job turns capability gaps into ranked coaching recommendations for managers (member/cohort, gap, priority, evidence, confidence, suggested action, effort).

title: Cost allocation job (CostAllocation, per-user, method + confidence)
requirement: A job allocates AI spend to users/teams with an allocationMethod and confidence, tracking allocated vs unallocated spend.

title: Manager insight generation + persisted feed with status lifecycle
requirement: A job generates ManagerInsight cards (category, severity, evidence, affected users/capabilities, recommended action) and persists a feed with a new/viewed/acted_on/dismissed lifecycle.

title: Auditability store for scores/actions
requirement: Store scoring version, calculation timestamp, evidence categories, confidence inputs, changes between score versions, manager actions, and dismissed recommendations.

title: Weekly Team Brief report
requirement: Generate a weekly manager brief: major changes, new risks, coaching priorities, emerging experts, data-quality issues.

title: Monthly Capability Review report
requirement: Generate a monthly review: team maturity, capability trends, member movement, gaps, coaching progress, concentration, spend context, priorities.

title: Quarterly Capability Review report
requirement: Generate a quarterly review: longer-term trend, role-pack development, coaching outcomes, skill coverage, operational cost trend, next-quarter priorities.

title: PDF export
requirement: Export reports and the capability matrix as PDF.

title: CSV export of underlying report/matrix tables
requirement: Export underlying tables (matrix, cost table, board numbers) as CSV.

title: Scheduled / configurable report email delivery
requirement: Deliver reports on a schedule via email, ideally user-configurable per report type.

title: Manager notification delivery + triggers
requirement: Deliver manager notifications (new maturity level, capability declined, several ready for training, expertise concentrated, plateau, cost up without capability growth, data source stopped syncing) with deep link + recommended action.

title: Member (individual) notification delivery
requirement: Deliver per-member notifications (new development priority, manager assigned coaching, mentor assigned, capability improved, recommendation ready, personal cost incomplete) with deep link.

title: Top-level 'Team Intelligence' nav with 11 sub-items
requirement: Add a new top-level product area 'Team Intelligence' with 11 gated sub-items (Overview, Capability Matrix, Team Members, Growth, Coaching, Skills & Experts, Costs & Efficiency, Insights, Benchmarks, Reports, Team Settings), hidden from non-managers.

title: Team Overview screen (aggregate summary)
requirement: A manager 30-second overview: overall team capability + maturity + confidence, capability distribution, universal capability profile, ≤4 priority insight cards, growth chart, coaching queue preview, cost snapshot, freshness indicator.

title: Team Overview header controls (selectors + filters)
requirement: Header with team selector, date-range selector, role filter, location/subgroup filter, compare-period toggle, export action, data-freshness indicator.

title: Universal capability profile (bars/radar) + single team capability score
requirement: Render the 7 universal capability dimensions as aligned bars (radar supplementary) with score/trend/confidence/benchmark, plus a single overall team capability score.

title: Capability Matrix (people × capabilities grid)
requirement: A grid of team members (rows) × capabilities (columns) with per-cell score/intensity/trend/confidence, hover explanations, click-through capability drawer, filters, column selector, CSV/PDF export, saved views.

title: Team Members roster with per-member capability columns
requirement: A sortable member table (overall capability, maturity, growth, strongest capability, development priority, coaching status, spend, last active) with expandable per-member rows and bulk coaching/mentor/note actions.

title: Individual Team Member Profile (manager-facing)
requirement: A manager-facing version of the individual growth profile with tabs (Overview, Capabilities, Skills, Growth, Cost/Usage, Coaching, Notes) for any named member, plus assign-coaching/mentor/note/export actions.

title: Team Growth screen (trend, movement, segments, coaching impact)
requirement: A team growth surface: capability trend line, capability-movement counts (up/down/stable/insufficient), growth segments (fastest/plateaued/declining/newly-active/inactive), coaching-impact pre/post, forecasting (labeled projection).

title: Coaching Center (manager assign/campaigns/mentor matches)
requirement: A manager surface to convert capability gaps into assigned coaching actions, run team campaigns, match mentors, and track actions through completion.

title: Skills & Experts (inventory + named expert directory)
requirement: A skills inventory (per-skill counts, maturity distribution, concentration risk) plus an Expert Directory listing named members with expertise, proficiency, recent evidence, and mentor availability.

title: Manager Insights feed (prioritized narrative + dismiss/convert)
requirement: A prioritized narrative feed of ManagerInsight cards (category, severity, explanation, confidence, evidence, recommended action, status) with filters and dismiss/save/convert-to-coaching actions.

title: Benchmarks screen (team/role/cohort comparisons)
requirement: Compare team vs previous period, team vs workspace average, role vs same-role average, subgroup vs parent, and (later) anonymized external benchmark, with population/period/confidence always shown and comparability guardrails.

title: Reports UI (weekly/monthly/quarterly + export)
requirement: Generate reusable manager reports (Weekly Team Brief, Monthly Capability Review, Quarterly Review) with in-app view, PDF, CSV export, and scheduled email delivery.

title: Shared: Capability Score Card + Capability Bar
requirement: A CapabilityScoreCard (score/level/trend/change/confidence/coverage/onExplain) and a Capability Bar (label/score/benchmark/trend/confidence).

title: Shared: Confidence Badge + Trend Indicator
requirement: A ConfidenceBadge (high/medium/low/insufficient data) and a TrendIndicator (improving/stable/declining/unavailable).

title: Shared: Insight Card variants
requirement: An Insight Card with variants positive/opportunity/warning/critical/disclosure.

title: Shared: Capability Detail Drawer
requirement: A drawer showing score, explanation, contributing signals, limiting signals, trend, confidence, recommendation, limitations.

title: Shared: Data Confidence Disclosure (progressive)
requirement: A progressive-disclosure data-confidence component: collapsed (plain summary, confidence status, impact) → expanded (technical reason, affected metrics, remediation, last-checked).

title: Shared: Member Avatar Row
requirement: A row showing avatar, name, role, status, and optional maturity badge for a member.

title: Shared: Empty State (honest, actionable)
requirement: An empty state that explains why data is unavailable, what is required, who can resolve it, and the expected effect after resolution.

title: Growth-over-ranking framing as UX law
requirement: The interface emphasizes improvement, readiness, strengths and coaching over employee competition.

title: No leaderboards / employee rankings
requirement: Avoid public leaderboards and simplistic employee rankings.

title: Per-score explainability (signals, change, evidence, limitations, next action)
requirement: Every capability score exposes contributing signals, confidence, recent changes, supporting evidence, known data limitations, and a recommended next action.

title: Never show a score without confidence + evidence availability
requirement: Capability/score values must never render without an attached confidence and evidence-availability indicator.

title: Insufficient-data / forming states never fabricate
requirement: Screens support an insufficient-data / forming state that explains the gap and never floors missing values to zero.

title: Structured empty state (why / what's required / who resolves / effect)
requirement: Empty states must explain why data is unavailable, what is required, who can resolve it, and the expected effect after resolution.

title: Low-confidence values visually distinct
requirement: Low-confidence and insufficient-data values are visually distinct across screens (cells, scores).

title: Data-confidence progressive disclosure
requirement: A collapsed plain-language confidence summary that expands to technical reason, affected metrics, remediation action, and last-checked time.

title: Individual capability SCORE as the hero metric
requirement: Keep the individual capability score as the hero metric on the IC overview.

title: IC overview section set (universal + role-pack breakdown, assigned coaching)
requirement: IC overview shows current capability, growth focus, universal capability breakdown, role-pack breakdown, recommendations, assigned coaching, personal cost, and data confidence.

title: Personal cost analytics on the IC view
requirement: Show monthly spend, spend trend, model mix, premium-model share, token usage, allocation confidence, and model-selection recommendations — without framing cost reduction as the objective.

title: Team context on the IC self-view (aggregates, priorities, mentors)
requirement: Where permitted, show the member how they compare to team aggregates, team capability priorities, available mentors, and chances to mentor others — without exposing named peer scores.

title: Flow A end-to-end: manager identifies gap → campaign
requirement: Manager opens Team Overview, sees weakest capability, opens capability detail with affected members + confidence, opens Coaching Center, creates a campaign, assigns a mentor, records a baseline, reviews progress.

title: Flow B end-to-end: find an internal expert
requirement: Manager opens Skills and Experts, searches a capability/tool, sees members ranked by proficiency + confidence, checks mentor availability, and assigns the expert.

title: Flow C end-to-end: investigate high spend per member
requirement: Manager opens Costs and Efficiency, filters for high spend, compares capability/growth/adoption/cost per member, distinguishes high-value advanced use from inefficiency, and assigns model-selection coaching only when evidence supports it.

title: Flow D end-to-end: individual reviews team-assigned development
requirement: Member opens Individual Overview, sees an assigned development priority, opens the score explanation, reviews recommendations + assigned activities, completes them, and the system tracks later capability change.

title: Dense controls (radar, matrix density/column toggles, 10+ filters) vs minimal-by-default
requirement: TCI team screens introduce radar charts, a capability-matrix density toggle + column selector + saved views, and 8-12 filter panels per screen.

title: Team Overview one-screen acceptance test
requirement: Prove a manager can read the team's strongest capability, weakest capability, growth direction, top coaching action, and total spend without leaving one screen.

title: Every score shows confidence + member coverage
requirement: Every displayed score must show a confidence level and the represented-member coverage (how many of the team the number is based on).

title: Every primary insight deep-links to detail
requirement: Every primary insight card must link to the supporting detail screen that explains it.

title: Capability Matrix — manager sees every member's per-capability scores
requirement: A manager can compare all members across all enabled capabilities in a people-by-capability grid.

title: Low-confidence / insufficient-data cells visually distinct + every cell explainable
requirement: Low-confidence and insufficient-data cells must be visually distinct, and every cell must open an explanation.

title: Coaching Center — insight→action, tracked to completion, baseline+follow-up stored
requirement: A manager can convert an insight into an assigned coaching action, track it through completion, and the system stores baseline and follow-up capability values.

title: Costs — separate high spend from adoption/capability; cost never in scoring
requirement: Managers can distinguish high spend from low adoption and from high-capability advanced use; cost-allocation confidence is visible; cost is never an input to capability scoring.

title: Manager-vs-member per-role authorization sweep (NEW test infra)
requirement: Users cannot access unauthorized individual data; each role (individual contributor, manager, admin, observer) sees only what its permissions allow.

title: Raw prompt content hidden by default
requirement: Raw prompt content must be hidden from managers by default.

title: Manager notes cannot affect algorithmic scoring
requirement: Manager-entered notes must never alter or feed into calculated capability scores.

title: Data limitations visible where they materially affect interpretation
requirement: Data limitations / confidence disclosures must be visible wherever incomplete data materially changes how a number should be read.

title: Three-registration discipline for every new team table
requirement: Every new org-scoped TCI table (Team, RolePack extensions, TeamCapabilitySnapshot, CostAllocation, CoachingCampaign, ManagerInsight, ManagerNote, mentor matches) must be registered in the three CI-enforced guards or it reds main.

title: MIN_PEOPLE floor tests for every new team aggregate
requirement: Every count-only team aggregate (capability distribution, maturity movement counts, coverage, concentration risk, benchmarks) must suppress any group below the MIN_PEOPLE floor entirely, with a test proving it.

title: Anti-gamification / banned-phrasing sweeps for all new team copy
requirement: New team/coaching/insight/benchmark/report copy must pass anti-gamification and no-leaderboard/no-ranking sweeps; benchmark screens must not become employee rankings.

title: Shared-source parity + migration-equivalence guards for team reuse
requirement: Where team aggregation, reports, notifications, and the dashboard reuse the same scoring/rec engine, tests must pin identical output (no silent divergence between surfaces).

title: Suite scale + flake tolerance for the expanded team suite
requirement: The test suite must stay reliable as the team layer roughly doubles it, tolerating the repo's known Windows/load-related flakes and keeping per-person query cost independent of team size.

title: TCI is an unsigned proposal, not ground truth
requirement: The TCI document must be filed and treated as an unratified proposal, with docs/Revealyst_Product_Spec_V4.md remaining product ground truth until the founder signs TCI off.

title: Gap analysis must follow the docs/product/ house style
requirement: The TCI gap analysis and any requirements registry must be authored in the established docs/product/ four-file house style, not as a free-form memo.

title: Superseded-banner + single-source-of-truth discipline
requirement: Any TCI plan/analysis doc must carry the repo's superseded-banner + single-source-of-truth convention so it can never silently outlive the code or duplicate ground truth.

title: Each new TCI entity needs an ADR + 3 registrations
requirement: Every net-new org-scoped table TCI implies (Team, RolePack, CapabilityDefinition, TeamCapabilitySnapshot, CostAllocation, CoachingCampaign, ManagerInsight, manager notes, etc.) must land via the repo's ADR + three-registration governance, with ADR and migration numbers claimed independently at build time.

title: Every conflicting TCI ask needs a product-signoffs row first
requirement: Each TCI requirement that crosses a founder-signed decision or named gate must get a docs/product-signoffs.md row (pending → ratified/overridden) before it is built.

title: Manager-facing per-person capability view crosses self-view-only
requirement: TCI's Capability Matrix and manager-facing individual profile expose each named member's per-capability scores to their manager.

title: Named per-person team scores cross count-only + MIN_PEOPLE
requirement: TCI shows named members in a matrix/table with individual scores and small-segment breakdowns, without a minimum-people floor.

title: Manager-visible individual cost crosses privacy model
requirement: TCI lets managers see each member's individual spend, model mix, and token volume.

title: Prompt-level evidence crosses the no-prompt-content tripwire
requirement: TCI implies prompt-level evidence can be made available (hidden 'by default', configurable on).

title: Eight non-engineering role packs cross NOT-015 + role-expansion gate
requirement: TCI ships Product, Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, HR, Finance, Operations role packs alongside Engineering.

title: Whole team product assumed live — blocked on W6-A dogfood clock
requirement: TCI assumes a manager-facing team product exists and can be built now.

title: 11-item nav tree crosses minimal-nav U0 IA + D4
requirement: TCI adds a top-level 'Team Intelligence' area with eleven sub-items (Overview, Capability Matrix, Team Members, Growth, Coaching, Skills & Experts, Costs & Efficiency, Insights, Benchmarks, Reports, Team Settings).

title: New inactive→expert ladder + 0–100 score cross 'no competing ladder'
requirement: TCI introduces a numeric 0–100 capability score plus a five-level 'inactive|beginner|intermediate|advanced|expert' ladder as the hero metric.

title: Repositioning to a manager-first platform crosses individual-first VIS-001
requirement: TCI repositions Revealyst as an 'AI Capability Intelligence and Growth Platform' with a manager-facing pitch.

title: Record the orchestrator conflict-resolution rule
requirement: The rule that conflicts resolve toward TCI only where TCI does not cross a founder-signed decision or named gate must be written down as the governing resolution rule for this analysis.
